_description: Meta-rules for predicting realistic review output per archetype. Used by review-predictor.js to generate realistic multilingual reviews of the simulated stay.

tripadvisor.tone_biases: long-form, narrative, mention of specific staff names and incidents

business_traveler_positive: Check-in was under 3 minutes, exactly what I need.
Wifi held up for a 90-minute video call, no drops.
Room was spotless and quiet on the 14th floor.
Breakfast grab-and-go at 6am was a lifesaver.
Late checkout granted without issue — reliable for business travel.
business_traveler_negative: Three people ahead of me at check-in, no queue management — took 14 minutes.
Hidden €28 daily resort fee not mentioned at booking.
HVAC cycled loudly every 20 minutes, couldn't sleep.
Wifi dropped during a client call, unacceptable.
Breakfast closes at 09:30 — useless for business travelers with 7am meetings.
family_vacationer_positive: Kids club staff were amazing — our two boys didn't want to leave.
Connecting rooms confirmed at booking and ready on arrival.
Buffet had something for everyone, including my picky 5-year-old.
Pool was clean, lifeguards attentive, and shallow end for little ones.
Chef prepared a special allergy-safe meal for my daughter — genuinely cared.
family_vacationer_negative: Connecting rooms not available despite being requested at booking.
Kids club was full by 10am — first-come first-served is terrible for planning.
Buffet repeated the exact same items all 7 days.
Pool chairs all reserved with towels by 9am — European chaos.
€45 daily resort fee for 'amenities' we already paid for — feels like bait-and-switch.
luxury_seeker_positive: The concierge remembered our preferences from arrival and had our usual table ready on night 3.
Service at the Michelin-star restaurant rivaled anything in Paris.
Our suite overlooked the Mediterranean, and the turndown service included a handwritten note from the butler.
This is how luxury should feel — quiet confidence, not performative.
The spa treatment was world-class — therapist was intuitive and the product quality exceptional.
luxury_seeker_negative: For €1,400 a night, I expected more than a generic 'Hello sir' at check-in.
The promotional 'weekend getaway from €89' banner on the Gran Meliá homepage cheapens the brand.
Room service menu was identical to the mid-tier property next door — chef, please differentiate.
Four Seasons sets a higher bar for the same price — this felt corporate-generic.
Staff clearly read from scripts. Where is the genuine hospitality?
honeymooner_positive: Walked into our room on arrival and found rose petals, champagne, and a handwritten note addressed to us.
The private beach dinner on our 5th night was unforgettable — staff disappeared tactfully after serving.
Couples spa treatment ended on a private terrace overlooking the ocean.
They remembered it was our honeymoon and surprised us with a cake on our last night.
Every detail felt intentional. We'll absolutely come back for our anniversary.
honeymooner_negative: We told them three times this was our honeymoon. No acknowledgment, no upgrade, no welcome drink. Heartbreaking.
Balcony faced a dirty service area — 'ocean view' was a sliver through construction.
Pool was dominated by families with loud children — we booked an adults-only escape.
Room smelled like cleaning chemicals rather than anything romantic.
The 'honeymoon package' we paid €300 extra for amounted to a cheap bottle of cava.
digital_nomad_positive: WiFi measured consistently at 180Mbps — good for Zoom, streaming, uploads.
Room had an actual ergonomic chair and a real desk, not a hotel-lobby armchair.
Monthly rate was transparent and matched the quoted price.
Staff respected 'Do Not Disturb' throughout my 3-week stay.
Laundry service was fairly priced and quick turnaround.
digital_nomad_negative: Advertised 'high-speed wifi'. Measured 12Mbps during daytime — unusable for calls.
No real desk, just a bar-height counter with an uncomfortable stool.
Housekeeping bangs on door at 09:30 every day despite the DND sign.
Thin walls — every phone call from the next room audible.
Advertised 'long-stay friendly' but monthly rate was hidden behind nightly pricing.
budget_optimizer_positive: Rate matched Booking.com exactly — no surprise fees at checkout.
Wifi was free and worked. Parking was free as promised.
Clean, functional room for the price. Nothing fancy but nothing broken.
Breakfast was actually included and had real food, not just cereal.
Checked all my charges at checkout — matched my booking to the cent.
budget_optimizer_negative: €28 'resort fee' appeared on my bill that was never disclosed at booking — I'm disputing with my credit card.
City tax of €4.50/night added at check-in without warning.
Parking was €25/day, not the 'complimentary' shown in the ad.
Upsell pressure at every interaction — staff clearly incentivized on add-ons.
Booking.com rate was €12/night cheaper — never booking direct again.
loyalty_maximizer_positive: Recognized by name as Platinum at check-in, upgrade to suite confirmed, welcome amenity in room.
Lounge access worked perfectly — food and beverage selection exceeded expectations.
Points posted to my account the morning after checkout.
Late checkout granted without asking — Meliá knows how to treat Platinum.
Personal touch from the duty manager on departure acknowledging my loyalty. This is why I stay 50+ nights a year.
loyalty_maximizer_negative: Platinum tier — not acknowledged at check-in. Had to ask about upgrade eligibility.
'Suite upgrade unavailable' despite booking.com showing 12 suites available at same rate.
Member rate did not auto-apply — had to manually toggle on every search.
Bonus points promo was not applied; requires post-stay follow-up with support.
Lounge access denied — 'system didn't flag your tier'. Had to escalate.
event_attendee_positive: Fast check-in despite a group of 20 arriving at the same time — well-organized.
Iron and ironing board in room was perfect condition — important for our corporate event.
Walking distance to IFEMA confirmed — took 7 minutes to the conference.
Late checkout granted so I could freshen up before my flight.
Hotel bar stayed open late — great for winding down after the event dinner.
event_attendee_negative: Group code not recognized at check-in, took 15 minutes with colleagues waiting behind.
Iron was broken — had to find one at reception, delayed preparation.
Shuttle to venue was late — missed the opening keynote.
Walls too thin for quiet call prep before my speech.
Hotel gym closed at 20:00 — impossible for event attendees.
